title: Dactylorhiza sambucina
uses: Dactylorhiza sambucina, also known as the elder orchid, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional European medicine, Dactylorhiza sambucina has been used to treat various conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems. Its roots are said to have a sedative effect and have been used to treat insomnia and anxiety. However, it's essential to note that the plant's medicinal uses are not extensively scientifically studied, and its effectiveness may vary.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza sambucina is a species of orchid in the genus Dactylorhiza. It is commonly known as the elder-flowered orchid due to its association with elder trees. The classification of Dactylorhiza sambucina is within the family Orchidaceae, which includes over 30,000 species of orchids. This orchid is further divided into several subspecies and varieties, with Dactylorhiza sambucina sambucina being the most widely recognized. As a member of the Dactylorhiza genus, Dactylorhiza sambucina is closely related to other orchids in the same genus.
benefits: Dactylorhiza sambucina, also known as the elder-flowered orchid, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help in soothing skin irritations and wounds. Its extract has also been studied for its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. constituents: Dactylorhiza sambucina contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides. These compounds have been found to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Flavonoids present in the plant, such as quercetin and kaempferol, may contribute to its medicinal effects. The phenolic acids and glycosides in Dactylorhiza sambucina are thought to be responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and their potential uses of Dactylorhiza sambucina.
